input: Give me a TL;DR on how RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) works, when to use it vs fine-tuning, and what the main failure modes are.
criteria: explains RAG in 2-3 sentences at most before going deeper
clearly distinguishes when RAG is better than fine-tuning (dynamic/external knowledge vs baked-in behavior)
lists at least 3 concrete failure modes (e.g., retrieval misses, context window overflow, hallucination from bad chunks)
structured with clear sections — not a wall of text
a developer could act on this immediately without additional research
judge_prompt: You are evaluating a technical summary written by an AI research assistant. Score 0-10. Full marks: TL;DR is genuinely short (under 50 words), RAG flow is correct, fine-tuning distinction is accurate and practically useful, at least 3 real failure modes named with brief explanation, structure is clear. Deduct 2 if fewer than 3 failure modes. Deduct 2 if fine-tuning distinction is vague or wrong.
